issue: SLM-242 (RSC-A06): Add a fail-closed property gate for every TwoTower weight and schedule vector
linear_context: Follow-up to SLM-237/238 (RSC-A01/A02) recursive-depth supervision fixes; this issue makes every numeric/schedule field on ModelBuildConfig and TwoTowerConfig fail closed at construction time rather than silently truncating, zeroing, or ignoring invalid values.

components_touched.model.recursive_denoiser: v10 no-bump (test expectations updated to match earlier raise)

new_module.responsibility: Typed primitive validators (finite_scalar, non_negative_scalar, positive_scalar, interval_scalar, exact_length_vector, non_empty_vector, finite_non_negative_vector, positive_sum_vector, normalized_probability_vector, strictly_increasing_sequence, unique_enum_sequence, paired_equal_length_sequences, supported_capability_requirement) plus config-level validation reports and ModelBuildConfig/TwoTowerConfig fail-closed gates.

checked_families: positive architectural scalars (d_model, n_heads, layers, batch_size, steps, ...)
non-negative disabled-by-zero counters (recursive_transition_layers, eval_every, loss_eval_every, suffix_rollback_window, ...)
mask_min / mask_max in [0, 1] with mask_min <= mask_max
slot_component_class_weights / lexeme priors / span priors finite and non-negative

TwoTowerConfig.notes: Generic numeric gate plus the architecture-aware recursive-depth supervision gate from SLM-237/238, now invoked at config construction time when weights or recursive_depth_aux_mode are set.

scripts/train_model.py: --recursive-depth-aux-mode choices: off, intermediate_only, all_depths, legacy_all_depths (default None)

coverage.primitive_validators: finite_scalar, non_negative_scalar, positive_scalar, interval_scalar, non_empty_vector, finite_non_negative_vector, positive_sum_vector, normalized_probability_vector, strictly_increasing_sequence, unique_enum_sequence
coverage.model_build_config_rejections: negative/NaN/inf weights, mask_min > mask_max, mask outside [0,1], unsorted LTR stages, non-positive LTR stage, bad diffusion length buckets
twotower_config_recursive_depth_defects: intermediate_only with R=1 and non-empty weights

tests.updated_tests_rationale: SLM-242 moves the recursive-depth supervision validation into TwoTowerConfig.__post_init__, so the same fail-closed invariant is now exercised at model construction time instead of inside training_loss().

recommended_next_iteration: No further work in this issue. The fail-closed gate is now part of the construction path for both ModelBuildConfig and TwoTowerConfig. Future schedule/weight field additions that follow the existing *_weight / *_stages / *_buckets naming conventions will be gated automatically by validate_numeric_config; fields that do not match those conventions should be added explicitly to validate_numeric_config's family checks.
